methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Multiply Labs is a Series A-stage biotech company that has raised $41M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 11 comparables across Biotech & Health: 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and Profluent, spanning seed through Series B stages.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-Progression analysis anchors to $85M in total raised and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $170M. Peer-Median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied given the absence of disclosed revenue for the target company.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $144.5M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $735M. This wide dispersion reflects the acute sensitivity of early-stage biotech valuations to clinical-stage risk, IP defensibility, and founder track record—factors materially difficult to normalize across a heterogeneous peer set. Stage-progression implies a more conservative entry point, while peer-median valuations reflect significant outlier effects from well-capitalized platforms like Eikon and Xaira. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technology readiness level, intellectual property portfolio strength, and burn trajectory relative to capital raised.
